sections.question_synthetic: Whether candidates may contribute their campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, candidates may contribute their campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates. The Commission reversed its prior advice (A-89-533) that had prohibited such transfers based on Section 85304, determining at its March 13, 1990 meeting that such transfers are permissible.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; question and conclusion synthesized from body text. No section headers present. Three related file numbers (A-90-231, A-90-232, A-90-233) addressed in single letter. OCR errors present in original document but did not impede extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether candidates may contribute their campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Yes, candidates may contribute their campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates. The Commission reversed its prior advice (A-89-533) that had prohibited such transfers based on Section 85304, determining at its March 13, 1990 meeting that such transfers are permissible.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that candidates may contribute campaign funds to ballot measure committees controlled by other candidates, reversing prior guidance from Advice Letter A-89-533. The determination addresses requests from Lt. Governor Leo Ucarthy, Assemblymember Tom Hayden, and Attorney General John Van de Kamp regarding contributions to their respective ballot measure committees.
